meta_title: For Students: user onboarding to Solve tutorial hell (Advanced Idea List)
meta_description: Learn how to user onboarding and overcome tutorial hell. A Advanced-level Idea List using Antigravity and Claude Code.

content.seo_title: For Students: User Onboarding to Solve Tutorial Hell
content.seo_description: Learn how to build advanced user onboarding to overcome tutorial hell. A professional guide using Antigravity and Claude Code for your portfolio.
content.answer_block: To escape tutorial hell, stop following step-by-step videos and start engineering real-world user flows. By building an advanced, state-driven user onboarding module, you master asynchronous state management, persistent data patterns, and UI/UX architecture—skills that instantly elevate your portfolio from student project to production-ready SaaS application.
content.intro:
The screen glows with the sterile light of another 'To-Do List' tutorial. You’ve built it, but you haven't learned it. You are trapped in the recursive loop of tutorial hell, where code is copied but never synthesized. The difference between a student who consumes and an engineer who builds is the ability to solve a complex, non-linear problem like user onboarding.

User onboarding is the heartbeat of any SaaS. It requires managing user state, handling database persistence, and crafting a psychological journey. By building this, you stop being a passive observer and start being an architect. This guide uses Antigravity and Claude Code to bypass the boilerplate and force you into the deep waters of true software engineering.

steps.title: Define the State Machine Schema
steps.description: Don't just write code; model the logic. Define the onboarding steps (Welcome, Profile, Preferences) as a state machine to ensure the user flow is unbreakable.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate a robust TypeScript state machine schema for a 3-step user onboarding flow, including transitions for 'next', 'back', and 'complete' states with validation logic.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Create a stateMachine.ts file implementing the schema generated by Antigravity.'

steps.title: Implement Asynchronous Persistence
steps.description: Use Claude Code to bridge your state machine with a database. Ensuring onboarding progress saves even if the user refreshes is a critical production-level skill.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Supabase-compatible service function that saves the current onboarding step to a user metadata table using optimistic updates.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Integrate the persistence service into the onboarding component using React Query.'

steps.title: Build Dynamic Step Components
steps.description: Create a modular component architecture where each onboarding step is a separate, isolated component injected via the state machine.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Create a factory component in React that renders the appropriate step component based on the current state machine value, including smooth CSS transitions.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Refactor the onboarding view to use the factory component pattern.'

steps.title: Add Intelligent Input Validation
steps.description: User input is the most common point of failure. Implement Zod validation to ensure data integrity before the user moves to the next step.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Generate Zod schemas for user profile data and implement a validation hook that blocks state transitions if the input is invalid.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Add Zod validation to the onboarding submission handler.'

steps.title: Final Integration and Testing
steps.description: Automate the testing of your onboarding flow to ensure no edge cases remain. If it works under pressure, it's ready for your portfolio.
steps.antigravity_prompt: Write a Playwright test suite that simulates a user completing the onboarding flow, including refreshing the page mid-process.
steps.claude_code_command: claude run 'Execute the Playwright test suite and fix any identified race conditions.'

pro_tips: Always build onboarding as a state machine, not a series of boolean flags, to avoid 'spaghetti state' bugs.
Implement 'optimistic UI' updates; the user should never feel the latency of your database write.
Force yourself to handle the 'User Aborted' state; most tutorials ignore what happens when a user quits halfway through.
Use Zod for runtime type safety, not just TypeScript interfaces, to catch data corruption before it hits your DB.
Add a 'skip' logic that gracefully saves current progress so users can return later—this is a high-value feature employers love.
content.next_steps: Once your onboarding flow is live, deploy it to a staging environment and conduct a user testing session with a peer to identify friction points.
content.conversion_block: You now possess the architecture to build features that actual companies pay for. Stop waiting for the perfect tutorial and start engineering your own solutions. Ready to build? Join our AI Builder community → Start your first project today.
